metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_229
metadata.comment: Rationale: This paper demonstrates localization of drebrin A in dendritic spines in the cerebral cortex using confocal and electron microscopy (figures 3,4, 5). This paper also presents data that Drebrin A co-sediments with other actin-binding proteins in the insoluble fraction (figure 6). And, immuno-EM labeling on synaptosomes showed Drebrin A labeling on filamentous materials that were associated with postsynaptic densities (figure 7). Finally, biochemical evidence is provided that drebrin interacts with actin and the actin-binding proteins gelsolin and myosin II (figure 8). Together these data indicate that Drebrin A is bound to the postsynaptic actin cytoskeleton.. Experimental description: from M&M: "Ten-week-old Wistar rats were perfused with 4% paraformaldehyde in 0.1 m phosphate buffer, pH 7.2.[.,..]"
